extraction.one_line_summary: BARGUIL Warren won from a breakaway on Bastille Day, sitting on QUINTANA/CONTADOR/LANDA who did the work before outsprinting them at the finish
tactical_patterns: FROOME attacked steepest sections of final climb multiple times to test form after losing yellow, ARU marked every move.
Team SKY sent LANDA forward in break rather than defending, forcing ARU's isolated Astana to chase without teammates.
BARGUIL tactically sat on QUINTANA/CONTADOR/LANDA's chase work in closing kilometers, preserving energy for final sprint.
FROOME attacked on descent after climb neutralized, pressuring ARU's descending skills when GC group split.

rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Attacked steepest sections and descent aggressively despite looking weak earlier, silencing doubts about form after losing yellow
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: neutral: Successfully marked all Froome attacks while completely isolated without Astana teammates, vulnerable tactical position going forward
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: Sent in breakaway by Sky as offensive tactic rather than defending, strong enough to hold GC riders
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: positive: Showed tactical maturity sitting on chasers' wheels despite motivation of Bastille Day win, fast enough to win sprint
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Recovered from previous day's struggles to finish second, active in late-race chase despite earlier form doubts
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: positive: Attacked from death's door condition earlier in Tour, third place shows resurgence after GC struggles
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Did massive pace work on final climb, pulled 45 seconds back from leaders in last 3km
